mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and falling inflation indicates robust consumer demand without price pressures, supporting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative rank IC over any 24-month rolling period or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during early-cycle phases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in >60% of observations above the threshold across multiple inflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, boosting consumer confidence and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to produce a statistically significant positive alpha over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal expanding residential investment and construction activity, which drives upstream industrial demand and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show a negative or zero correlation with housing starts across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in >50% of triggered cases over a 4-year rolling window.
